expectations.text: Response treats the request as an architecture comparison task even without an explicit skill name
expectations.evidence: Detected 3 option/design sections for an implicit architecture request.

expectations.text: Response grounds the designs in the existing plan and task workflow files
The architecture options differ in orchestration or state ownership, not just naming
Comparison covers testing or migration implications in addition to feature shape
expectations.evidence: Comparison covers testing/review and migration/resume implications.

uncertainties: A saved plan means the normal scratchpad artifact set rooted at .agents/scratchpad/<feature>/, especially plan.md and tasks.md.
tasks.md remains the authoritative completion and resumption ledger; any new execution state is derived and must reconcile back to tasks.md.
Checkpoint reviews should reuse the existing code-simplifier and code-reviewer agents and preserve the current controller-owned review-scope rules.
Durable progress state should live next to scratchpad artifacts, not in workspace outputs or a new database layer.
